sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skillFeedback reports followedProcedure: true but also lists a deviation (skipping Steps 3-6). The skill's Special Cases section explicitly sanctions this for gate/verification features, so this is not a real deviation — the skill could be clearer by making the gate shortcut a named alternative procedure rather than a 'skip steps' exception.

summary: Verification-only gate passed. Worker correctly identified this as a no-code-changes feature and followed the skill's gate shortcut. All 5 required checks were executed and passed: (1) Full test suite: 1336 tests, 0 failures — 393 new tests above the 943 baseline, covering all 8 milestones. (2) Kernel build: cargo build --release clean. (3) M1 bug fix regression: 13 specific tests across 3 test groups (channel_create_leak, sched_context_create_leak, handle_send_rollback) all pass. (4) Miri cross-area (VAL-CROSS-002): Miri found zero kernel UB, so no UB patterns needed incorporation into audits — valid no-op outcome. (5) Audit→fuzzing cross-area (VAL-CROSS-003): Worker verified that audit-identified patterns (PA validation, dealloc routing, overflow, guard pages) are exercised in adversarial test files. Commit 59332c1 (HEAD) correctly referenced as the verification point — no new commit needed for a verification gate. All 5 fulfills satisfied: VAL-INTEG-003, VAL-INTEG-004, VAL-CROSS-001, VAL-CROSS-002, VAL-CROSS-003.
